name: Dumbbell Front Raise
resistance_profile: With dumbbells the front delt is barely loaded at the bottom and works hardest as the arm approaches horizontal, biasing the shortened (top) portion.
cues: Lift with the shoulder, not by leaning back
Lower slowly, resisting all the way down
execution_steps: Stand holding a dumbbell in each hand in front of the thighs, palms facing you.
Brace the trunk and keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Raise the dumbbells forward and up to about shoulder height, thumbs up or neutral.
Pause briefly at the top without swinging.
Lower under control back to the thighs and repeat.
good_when: The front delt needs direct isolation on top of pressing
Adding pump or finisher volume for the shoulders
You want to raise one arm at a time to focus each side
bad_when: Your front delts already get heavy pressing volume
You cannot avoid swinging the weight up
Raising to the front aggravates shoulder impingement
common_errors: Swinging with the hips and momentum
Going too heavy and shortening the range
Shrugging the traps to help lift
progressions: Switch to a cable for constant tension
regressions: Alternate arms to lighten each rep
Use lighter dumbbells and slow the tempo
